studyName: ECLS Survival in Pediatric HCT Recipient with Presumed Cardiac GVHD

tables.description: This subject phenotype data table includes the patient's sex, race, disease onset age, hematopoietic cellular transplant (HCT) indication and type, cell source, and number of HLA matches.

variables.name: Cell_Source
variables.description: How was the cellular transfusion mobilized from the donor

variables.name: HCT_Indication
variables.description: Reason for undergoing hematopoietic cellular transplantation

variables.name: HLA_Match
variables.description: Number of human leukocyte antigen alleles matched between donor and recipient

tables.description: This sample attributes table includes body site where sample was collected, analyte type, tumor status, and sequencing center.

variables.name: BODY_SITE
variables.description: Body site where sample was collected

variables.name: SEQUENCING_CENTER
variables.description: Name of the center which conducted sequencing

tables.description: The subject consent data table contains patient IDs, consent group information, and affection status.

variables.name: AFFECTION_STATUS
variables.description: Case control status of the subject for hypodiploid pre-B acute lymphoblastic leukemia status post matched unrelated donor allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant complicated by fulminant myocarditis on day +75

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

tables.description: This subject sample mapping data table includes a mapping of study patient IDs to sample IDs. Samples are the final preps submitted for genotyping, sequencing, and/or expression data. For example, if one patient (subject ID) gave one sample, and that sample was processed differently to generate 2 sequencing runs, there would be two rows, both using the same subject ID, but having 2 unique sample IDs. The data table also includes sample use.
